title: ipv6_addr.c logging abstraction enables host-side testing
description:
In ipv6_addr.c:20-33, a simple logging abstraction is used:

```c
/*
 * Logging abstraction: use Zephyr logging when available, otherwise
 * fall back to no-ops. This allows the module to be used in host-side
 * unit tests without stubbing the logging system.
 */
#ifdef __ZEPHYR__
#include <zephyr/logging/log.h>
LOG_MODULE_REGISTER(lichen_ipv6, LOG_LEVEL_INF);
#else
#define LOG_ERR(...)  ((void)0)
#define LOG_WRN(...)  ((void)0)
#define LOG_INF(...)  ((void)0)
#define LOG_DBG(...)  ((void)0)
#endif
```

This is a clean way to make the module testable in host unit tests without pulling in Zephyr dependencies or requiring complex mocking. The no-op macros are cast to (void)0 which silences unused-expression warnings if used in expression context.
close_reason: Positive finding - no action needed, documents good practice
